engineIntelligence.h2: Porsche 911 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Porsche 911 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Porsche 911 M96.05 engine - petrol 3.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: IMS bearing failure - intermediate shaft bearing can fail between 60k-100k miles causing catastrophic damage.
Rear main seal leak - oil seepage common on higher-mileage units requiring gasket replacement.
Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides degrade leading to rattle and potential chain skip.

cards.altText: Porsche 911 M97.01 engine - petrol 3.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Cylinder bore scoring - Alusil cylinder liners can score causing compression loss and oil consumption.
IMS bearing failure risk - carries over from M96 design requiring preventative upgrade consideration.
Air oil separator failure - PCV system degradation leads to excessive oil consumption and smoke.
Rod bearing wear - track-focused use accelerates bearing fatigue requiring Mahle upgrade.
Dry-sump seal degradation - high-G cornering stresses oil system seals over time.
VarioCam solenoid failure - variable valve timing components can stick causing performance loss.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £8,000-£15,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 9A1 units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Porsche 911 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Porsche 911's flat-six engines are engineering icons, but specific generations face known challenges. The M96/M97 series (996/997) is prone to IMS bearing failure and bore scoring, while later MA1/9A1 units can experience timing chain tensioner wear and DFI carbon buildup. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: IMS Bearing Failure - Why M96/M97 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The intermediate shaft bearing uses a sealed design that can degrade without warning, allowing metal fragments to circulate and cause catastrophic bottom-end damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove gearbox, replace IMS bearing with ceramic upgrade kit
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only before failure; prevents catastrophic damage if caught early

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt M96/M97 unit with IMS upgrade, upgraded components, bench testing
repairOptions.longevity: Restores reliability; includes 12-month warranty; best value post-failure

problems.recommendation: On a 996/997 Carrera worth £8,000-£25,000, preventative IMS upgrade is excellent value if mileage is under 80k. Post-failure, a rebuilt engine with IMS upgrade is more cost-effective than dealer repair and restores long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M96/M97 engine prices for your Porsche 911

problems.h4: Cylinder Bore Scoring - Alusil Liner Wear on M97/MA1 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Porsche 911 997.1 Carrera S, 997.2 Carrera S (2005-2012, M97.01 & MA1.01)
problems.rootCause: Alusil cylinder liners can develop scoring from piston skirt contact, especially with oil starvation or aggressive cold starts, leading to compression loss and excessive oil consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, hone bores, fit Nikasil-coated pistons, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life if scoring is mild; not viable for deep scoring

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit reconditioned M97/MA1 with Alusil reconditioning or sleeved cylinders
repairOptions.longevity: Restores compression and oil control; includes warranty; only option for severe scoring

problems.recommendation: If compression tests show >15% variance or oil consumption exceeds 1L/1,000 miles, bore scoring is likely advanced. A reconditioned engine with Alusil reconditioning is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M97.01 bore-scoring fix for your Porsche 911

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Rattle on 991/992 Models
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioners can lose pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes metallic rattling on cold start and risks chain skip or valve timing errors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioners and chain guides; no engine removal required
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early before chain stretch; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt 9A1/MA1 unit with updated tensioners and chain components
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if rattling has persisted; includes warranty and upgraded components

problems.recommendation: If you hear cold-start rattling that disappears after 2-3 seconds, replace tensioners immediately. If rattling persists when warm, chain stretch may have occurred - a rebuilt engine is safer and more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 9A1.20 engine prices for your Porsche 911

problems.h4: Turbo Wastegate Rattle - VTG Actuator Wear on Turbo Models
problems.rootCause: Variable turbine geometry actuators develop play in linkage arms, causing metallic rattling on deceleration and potentially affecting boost control accuracy.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbos, replace wastegate arms and bushings, recalibrate VTG
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early-stage rattle; requires specialist calibration equipment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt MA1.70/9A1.70 with upgraded turbo center cartridges and actuators
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Turbos; includes warranty and boost calibration on dyno

problems.recommendation: For Turbos under 70k miles with mild rattle, actuator replacement is cost-effective. For units over 80k miles or with boost errors, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbos offers better long-term value and performance consistency.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt MA1.70 Turbo engine prices for your Porsche 911

problems.h4: DFI Carbon Buildup - Intake Valve Deposits on MA1/9A1 Engines
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design means fuel doesn't wash intake valves, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate and restrict airflow, causing rough idle, misfires, and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast valves with walnut media, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Effective maintenance procedure; recommended every 40k miles for DFI engines

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Port Injection Retrofit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt MA1/9A1 with optional port injection retrofit to prevent future buildup
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage DFI engines; retrofit eliminates future carbon issues

problems.recommendation: Walnut blasting is essential maintenance for DFI 911s - budget for it every 40k miles. If valves are severely pitted or engine has misfire codes, a rebuilt engine with port injection retrofit prevents recurrence.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MA1.01 carbon-clean rebuild for your Porsche 911

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Porsche 911
finalCta.paragraph: The Porsche 911's engine challenges are well-documented, but they don't have to end your ownership journey. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand IMS bearings, bore scoring, and turbo wastegates intimately. Every rebuilt or reconditioned engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from trusted Porsche 911 specialists today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Porsche 911 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Porsche 911 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Porsche 911 Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Porsche 911 engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Porsche 911 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Fully disassembled, machined to factory specs, with upgraded components addressing known failure points like ims bearings.
types.frontDescription: Fully disassembled, machined to factory specs, with upgraded components addressing known failure points like ims bearings.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code, variant and mileage.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Porsche 911 engines undergo complete teardown, cylinder honing or sleeving, and component replacement. Ideal for M96/M97 units needing ims upgrade or bore scoring prevention.

types.title: Porsche 911 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Core engine refurbished with critical wear items replaced; cost-effective solution for engines with moderate wear or single-point failures.
types.frontDescription: Core engine refurbished with critical wear items replaced; cost-effective solution for engines with moderate wear or single-point failures.
types.frontDisclaimer: Suitable for engines with verifiable service history.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Porsche 911 engines focus on replacing failed components while retaining sound internals. Common for timing chain tensioner fixes or turbo actuator replacements on 991/992 models.
backBullets: Critical wear parts replaced as standard

types.title: Porsche 911 Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage donor engines from accident-damaged 911s; budget option when verified compression and service history are confirmed.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage donor engines from accident-damaged 911s; budget option when verified compression and service history are confirmed.
types.frontDisclaimer: Limited warranty; verify compression before purchase.
types.backDescription: Used Porsche 911 engines offer immediate availability for urgent replacements. Best for late-model 991/992 units with documented service records and no failure history.

types.title: Porsche 911 Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-spec rebuild using new or reconditioned components; highest reliability option for track-focused GT3 or high-mileage Carrera units.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec rebuild using new or reconditioned components; highest reliability option for track-focused GT3 or high-mileage Carrera units.
types.frontDisclaimer: Premium option with extended warranty available.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured Porsche 911 engines meet or exceed original factory tolerances. Essential for GT3/GT2 track applications requiring dry-sump validation and Mahle bearing upgrades.

types.title: Porsche 911 Refurbished Engines
types.description: Cosmetic and functional refresh for engines with minor wear; includes gasket renewal, sensor calibration and fluid service.
types.frontDescription: Cosmetic and functional refresh for engines with minor wear; includes gasket renewal, sensor calibration and fluid service.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best for engines with good compression and no major faults.
types.backDescription: Refurbished Porsche 911 engines address surface wear and minor leaks without full teardown. Suitable for preventative maintenance on well-serviced M97/MA1 units before high-mileage use.

types.title: Porsche 911 Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Complete engine replacement service including removal, installation, calibration and road test by Porsche-specialist garages nationwide.
types.frontDescription: Complete engine replacement service including removal, installation, calibration and road test by Porsche-specialist garages nationwide.
types.frontDisclaimer: Labour cost varies by location and garage rates.
types.backDescription: Supply & fit Porsche 911 services include engine removal, installation, DME coding, coolant flush and post-fit road test. Ideal for owners without specialist tools or garage access.
backBullets: Post-fit warranty on labour typically 12 months
types.cta: Find Porsche 911 engine supply & fit deals

engineSizes.h2: Porsche 911 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Porsche 911 is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Porsche 911 3.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.4L M96.01 powered early 996 Carrera models (1997-2001), establishing Porsche's water-cooled flat-six era. While offering smooth power delivery, this displacement is most associated with early IMS bearing vulnerability requiring preventative attention.
commonFailurePoints: bore scoring risk on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 911 3.4L petrol engines

items.title: Porsche 911 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L M96.05 and M97.01 became the backbone of the 996/997 Carrera range (2002-2008), balancing performance and refinement. This displacement remains the most searched for replacement due to IMS and bore scoring concerns across both generations.

items.title: Porsche 911 3.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.8L M97.01 and MA1.01 powered Carrera S and early Turbo variants (2005-2012), delivering enhanced torque and top-end power. Direct injection introduction in MA1.01 brought carbon buildup considerations alongside improved efficiency.

items.title: Porsche 911 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L 9A1.20 and MA1.20 represent Porsche's modern turbocharged era (2012-Present), delivering strong low-end torque across 991 and 992 generations. Downsizing improved efficiency but introduced timing chain tensioner monitoring requirements.

items.title: Porsche 911 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L M97.76 and 9A1.24 power GT3 and GT3 RS variants (2006-Present), representing Porsche's naturally aspirated track-focused engineering. Dry-sump lubrication and Mahle bearing upgrades address high-G track demands.

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Porsche 911 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Porsche 911 specialists.

items.descriptor: All Porsche 911 models use naturally aspirated or twin-turbo petrol flat-six engines; no diesel variants were ever produced for this iconic sports car range.
families: M96 - 996 base Carrera (1997-2005), known for IMS bearing vulnerability requiring preventative upgrade
M97 - 997.1 Carrera S/Turbo (2005-2008), introduced bore scoring concerns on Alusil cylinders
MA1 - 997.2/991 transition (2009-2016), DFI introduction brought carbon buildup considerations
9A1 - 991/992 modern era (2012-Present), turbo downsizing with timing chain monitoring needs
knownFor: Exceptional flat-six character and high-revving responsiveness across all generations
IMS bearing failure risk on M96/M97 engines between 60k-100k miles
Bore scoring vulnerability on M97.01 Alusil cylinders requiring specialist reconditioning
DFI carbon buildup on MA1/9A1 engines needing periodic walnut blasting maintenance

engineYears.h2: Porsche 911 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Porsche 911 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The water-cooled 996 introduced the M96 flat-six, marking Porsche's departure from air-cooled engineering and bringing IMS bearing concerns.
keyChanges: First water-cooled 911 engine (M96 series) replacing air-cooled units
Introduction of intermediate shaft bearing design later linked to failures
Base Carrera launched with 3.4L M96.01, later updated to 3.6L
knownFor: IMS bearing failure risk dominates replacement enquiries for this generation
Smooth power delivery but requires vigilant maintenance history verification
Alusil cylinder liners susceptible to bore scoring on higher-mileage examples
Strong enthusiast community support for preventative upgrade solutions
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-2001 Porsche 911 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 911 IMS bearing replacement • Porsche 911 M96 engine rebuild • Porsche 911 996 Carrera engine cost

years.description: Updated M96.05 brought displacement increase and minor revisions, but IMS vulnerability remained the defining concern for UK owners.
keyChanges: M96.05 introduced with revised internals and 3.6L displacement across Carrera range
Improved cooling system design but IMS bearing design unchanged
Final 996 production years before 997 transition
knownFor: Peak IMS failure enquiry period for 996 models in UK marketplace
Strong aftermarket support for LN Engineering ceramic IMS retrofit kits
Bore scoring concerns emerge on high-mileage Alusil cylinder examples
Excellent value proposition for preventative rebuilds on well-serviced units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2005 Porsche 911 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 911 M96.05 replacement • Porsche 911 996 bore scoring fix • Porsche 911 IMS upgrade cost

years.description: The 997.1 brought styling refinement and the M97 engine family, with Carrera S models gaining 3.8L displacement but inheriting bore scoring vulnerability.
keyChanges: M97 series introduced: M97.01 (3.8L NA) for Carrera S, M97.02 (3.8L TT) for Turbo
Alusil cylinder liner technology expanded, bringing bore scoring concerns
Turbo models gained VTG (variable turbine geometry) turbochargers
knownFor: Bore scoring on M97.01 Alusil cylinders drives significant replacement demand
Turbo wastegate rattle emerges as common enquiry for M97.02 units
GT3 M97.76 highly regarded but track use accelerates rod bearing wear
Strong collector interest in late 997.1 models with verified service history
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2008 Porsche 911 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 911 M97.01 bore scoring • Porsche 911 Turbo wastegate rattle • Porsche 911 GT3 rod bearing upgrade

years.description: The 997.2 facelift introduced direct injection (MA1 series), improving efficiency but bringing carbon buildup considerations for intake valves.
keyChanges: MA1 engine family launched with direct fuel injection (DFI) across Carrera range
Timing chain tensioner design updated but monitoring still recommended
Turbo models gained revised VTG actuators and improved thermal management
knownFor: DFI carbon buildup on intake valves requires periodic walnut blasting maintenance
Improved reliability over M97 but timing chain tensioner wear still monitored
Turbo models benefit from revised VTG calibration reducing wastegate rattle frequency
GTS models highly sought after for balanced performance and relative affordability
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 Porsche 911 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 911 DFI carbon clean • Porsche 911 MA1.01 rebuild • Porsche 911 GTS engine replacement

years.description: The 991 introduced turbocharged base engines (9A1.20) and longer wheelbase chassis, with timing chain tensioner monitoring becoming a key maintenance focus.
keyChanges: 9A1 engine family: turbocharged 3.0L for base Carrera, naturally aspirated 3.8L/4.0L for GT models
Longer wheelbase and revised chassis dynamics across all variants
DME software updates improved drivability but added calibration complexity
knownFor: Timing chain tensioner wear on early 991 Carrera models drives replacement enquiries
GT3 9A1.24 highly regarded but rod bearing upgrades recommended for track use
Turbo models benefit from improved thermal management over 997 predecessors
Strong demand for verified service history on higher-mileage 991 examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2016 Porsche 911 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 911 9A1.20 tensioner fix • Porsche 911 GT3 rod bearing • Porsche 911 991 Turbo rebuild

years.description: The 991.2 brought turbocharged engines across the entire Carrera range, with revised 9A1 variants and improved thermal management systems.
keyChanges: Base Carrera models switched to turbocharged 3.0L (9A1.20) from naturally aspirated
GT3 received updated 4.0L 9A1.24 with Mahle bearing upgrades per Porsche TSB
Turbo S gained 580hp output with revised charge air cooling
knownFor: Turbocharged base engines improved low-end torque but retained DFI carbon considerations
GT3 9A1.24 rod bearing upgrades per Porsche SWE-19-07 TSB highly recommended
Turbo S models benefit from revised intercooler design reducing heat soak
Strong enthusiast preference for 991.2 GT3 as final naturally aspirated 4.0L iteration
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Porsche 911 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 911 991.2 turbo rebuild • Porsche 911 GT3 Mahle bearing • Porsche 911 DFI walnut blast

years.description: The 992 introduced the latest MA1.20/MA1.25 turbo engines with revised DME calibration, wider track and enhanced digital integration across all variants.
keyChanges: MA1.20/MA1.25 turbo engines with updated thermal management and DME software
Wider rear track and revised suspension geometry across Carrera range
Digital cockpit and enhanced driver assistance systems standard
knownFor: Early 992 turbo actuator wear reports on MA1.20 units under warranty
DFI carbon buildup remains maintenance consideration requiring walnut blasting
Strong demand for verified service history on higher-mileage examples
Latest DME calibration improves drivability but requires specialist diagnostic tools
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-Present Porsche 911 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 911 992 turbo actuator • Porsche 992 Carrera rebuild • Porsche 911 MA1.20 replacement

faq.h2: Porsche 911 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Porsche 911 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Porsche 911 engine replacement costs range from £3,500 for rebuilt M96 units to £15,000+ for GT3 track-spec engines, depending on variant, engine code and condition. Supply-only pricing excludes fitting labour.
keyPoints: Base Carrera M96/M97 rebuilt: £3,500-£8,500 supply only
Turbo MA1.70/9A1.70 rebuilt: £6,000-£12,500 supply only
GT3 9A1.24 track-spec rebuilt: £8,000-£15,000+ supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £1,200-£3,500 depending on garage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Porsche 911 engine prices for your variant

items.question: What is the most reliable Porsche 911 engine?
items.answer: Post-2009 MA1 and 9A1 engines show improved reliability over M96/M97 units when properly maintained, with DFI carbon management and timing chain monitoring being key maintenance priorities.
keyPoints: M96/M97 (pre-2009) require IMS bearing monitoring and bore scoring checks
MA1/9A1 (2009+) benefit from revised internals but need DFI carbon cleaning
GT3 naturally aspirated units generally more robust than turbocharged counterparts
Full service history and specialist maintenance significantly extend engine life
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable MA1/9A1 Porsche 911 replacement engines

items.question: Can I replace my Porsche 911 engine with a different code?
items.answer: Engine code swaps are possible but require DME recoding, wiring harness adaptation and potentially gearbox modifications; compatibility depends on chassis generation and variant.
keyPoints: Same-generation swaps (e.g., M97.01 to M97.02) simpler than cross-generation
DME software must be adapted to match new engine code and variant
Turbo to NA or vice versa swaps require extensive supporting modifications
Always verify compatibility via VIN/engine code lookup before proceeding
items.cta: Check Porsche 911 engine code compatibility for your variant

items.question: How long does a Porsche 911 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 3-7 days for common codes; specialist supply & fit installations require 2-4 days garage time depending on variant complexity.
keyPoints: Common Carrera engines (M96/M97/MA1) often available within 3-5 days
Turbo and GT3 specialist units may require 7-14 days sourcing time
Specialist garage installation typically 2-3 days for Carrera, 3-4 for Turbo/GT3
Post-fit DME adaptation and road test add half-day to completion timeline
items.cta: Book your Porsche 911 engine supply & fit slot today

items.question: What warranty comes with a Porsche 911 reconditioned engine?
items.answer: All reconditioned Porsche 911 engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network suppliers
Warranty covers internal components, seals, bearings and machining defects
Labour for warranty claims typically covered at installing garage
Always confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before order confirmation
items.cta: Compare Porsche 911 engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: Do you supply Porsche 911 engines for track use?
items.answer: Yes, GT3 and GT2 track-spec rebuilt engines are available with dry-sump validation, Mahle bearing upgrades and dyno testing to meet circuit demands.
keyPoints: GT3 9A1.24/M97.76 rebuilt with dry-sump pressure validation
Mahle rod bearing upgrades fitted per Porsche SWE-19-07 TSB
Dyno testing to factory power specs before dispatch
Track-use warranty terms may differ; confirm with supplier
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche 911 GT3 track-spec engine availability

items.question: How do I know which engine code my Porsche 911 has?
items.answer: Your engine code is stamped on the crankcase near the oil filter housing, visible via VIN decoder tools, or confirmed instantly by entering your registration above.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on crankcase (left side, near oil filter)
VIN decoder tools can identify factory-fitted engine code
Service records and Porsche Certificate of Authenticity list engine code
Enter registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine identification
items.cta: Identify your Porsche 911 engine code instantly

items.question: Is it better to rebuild or replace a Porsche 911 engine?
items.answer: Replacement with a rebuilt/reconditioned unit is usually more cost-effective and reliable than repairing a failed engine, especially for IMS or bore scoring failures.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing original failure points
Repair costs often approach replacement value for catastrophic failures
Rebuilt units come with warranty; repairs typically do not
Time off road is shorter with pre-built replacement engines
items.cta: Compare rebuild vs replace costs for your Porsche 911

items.question: Do you offer Porsche 911 engine supply and fit?
items.answer: Yes, our UK network includes Porsche-specialist garages offering nationwide supply & fit services with DME calibration and post-installation road testing.
keyPoints: Supply & fit available nationwide through vetted specialist garages
Service includes engine removal, installation, DME coding and road test
Labour costs typically £1,200-£3,500 depending on variant and location
Book fitting slot concurrently with engine order to minimise downtime
items.cta: Find Porsche 911 engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What is included in a Porsche 911 reconditioned engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines undergo full teardown, machining to factory specs, replacement of wear items, upgraded components for known failure points, bench testing and warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Complete disassembly, cleaning and dimensional inspection
Cylinder honing/sleeving, new pistons, bearings and seals fitted
Upgraded components for known issues (IMS, tensioners, etc.)
Bench-tested and dyno-verified before dispatch with warranty
items.cta: View what's included in your Porsche 911 reconditioned engine
